evals.name: full-sweep

assertions.trap: Order exposes internalDbRowId and _sequelizeTimestamp to clients
assertions.description: Minimize observable surface — every field exposed is a commitment that cannot be safely removed

assertions.trap: Error handler returns err.stack; ship endpoint returns queryPlan and internalDbRowId when internal=true
assertions.description: Never leak implementation details like stack traces, internal IDs, or query plans to clients

assertions.trap: v2 exists alongside v1 with no deprecation headers on v1 endpoints
assertions.description: Plan deprecation at design time — add Deprecation and Sunset response headers before removing anything

assertions.trap: OrderV2 is a forked copy of Order instead of extending the original with optional fields
assertions.description: Avoid parallel versions — extend with optional fields instead of creating v2 types

assertions.trap: No typed input/output interfaces exist; handlers use raw req.body with no contract separating input from output
assertions.description: Define TypeScript interfaces for input and output before implementation — separate input from output, server-generated fields only in output

assertions.trap: createOrder returns { message, stack }, getOrder returns { msg }, other endpoints have no error handling
assertions.description: One error shape everywhere — { error: { code, message, details? } } with no exceptions across endpoints

assertions.trap: createOrder returns 200 instead of 201; all errors in createOrder return 500 even for validation failures (400/422)
assertions.description: Use correct HTTP status codes — 201 for creation, 400 for bad request, 422 for semantic errors, 500 only for unexpected server errors

assertions.trap: createOrder throws exceptions, getOrder returns a JSON error, put handler throws unhandled, ship returns a success boolean
assertions.description: Never mix error handling patterns — pick one strategy and enforce it everywhere

assertions.trap: createOrder is at /api/createOrder with no version prefix while other endpoints use /v1/
assertions.description: Default to URL versioning (/v1/) consistently across all endpoints

assertions.trap: GET /api/v1/orders returns a raw array with no pagination metadata
assertions.description: Always paginate list endpoints with proper pagination metadata (cursor or offset-based)

assertions.trap: v2 orders returns { items, page, size } missing totalItems and totalPages for offset pagination
assertions.description: Offset pagination must include totalItems and totalPages; use 'data' as the collection key for consistency

assertions.trap: OrderV2 removes internalDbRowId and _sequelizeTimestamp fields present in Order
assertions.description: Add, never remove — removed fields break consumers (Hyrum's Law)

assertions.trap: priority is string in Order but number in OrderV2
assertions.description: Never change field types — changing priority from string to number is a breaking change

assertions.trap: OrderV2 uses nullable trackingNumber, cancelledReason, shippedAt instead of discriminated unions per status
assertions.description: Use discriminated unions for variants — each state carries exactly the fields it needs, no nullable fields that only exist in certain statuses

assertions.trap: Order uses plain string for id and userId instead of branded OrderId and UserId types
assertions.description: Use branded types for IDs — OrderId and UserId must be distinct types to prevent misuse

assertions.trap: calculateTotal calls validateOrder internally — validation between internal functions sharing type contracts
assertions.description: Validate at boundaries only — trust internal code, validate only where external input enters (API handlers)

assertions.trap: /api/createOrder uses a verb in the URL instead of POST /api/v1/orders
assertions.description: No verbs in REST URLs — use HTTP methods with noun resources (POST /api/v1/orders, not /api/createOrder)

assertions.trap: GET /api/v1/orders/:id/cancel uses GET for a state-changing operation instead of POST or DELETE
assertions.description: Do not use GET for state-changing operations — cancel should be POST or DELETE

assertions.trap: PUT /api/v1/orders/:id is used for what clients actually want as partial update
assertions.description: Use PATCH where clients want partial updates — PUT implies full replacement

assertions.trap: Ship endpoint uses ?internal=true query param to branch response shape
assertions.description: Boolean params that control branching are a red flag — split into named endpoints instead

assertions.trap: processThirdPartyWebhook uses payload as any with no runtime validation
assertions.description: Third-party API responses and webhooks must be validated at the boundary — never trust external input

assertions.trap: Ship endpoint returns { shipped: true } or { shipped: true, internalDbRowId, queryPlan } depending on query param
assertions.description: Endpoints must not return different shapes depending on conditions — response schema must be stable
